text: Why was the Ambassador particularly lucky?
When the Ambassador of Escalopia returned home for lunch, his wife got a shock.
zh: 埃斯卡罗皮亚大使回家吃午饭时,他的妻子大吃一惊。

text: He looked pale and his clothes were in a frightful state.
zh: 他脸色苍白,衣服也脏得可怕。

text: 'What has happened?' she asked.
'How did your clothes get into such a mess?'
'A fire extinguisher, my dear,' answered the Ambassador drily.
zh: “是灭火器弄的,亲爱的,”大使冷冷地答道。

text: 'University students set the Embassy on fire this morning.'
zh: “今天早上大学生放火烧了大使馆。”

text: 'Good heavens!' exclaimed his wife.
'And where were you at the time?'
'I was in my office as usual,' answered the Ambassador.
zh: “我像往常一样在办公室里,”大使答道。

text: 'The fire broke out in the basement.
I went down immediately, of course, and that fool, Horst,
zh: 我当然立刻下楼去了,结果那个傻瓜霍斯特,

text: aimed a fire extinguisher at me.
He thought I was on fire.
I must definitely get that fellow posted.'
zh: 我一定要把那家伙调走。”

text: The Ambassador's wife went on asking questions,
when she suddenly noticed a big hole in her husband's hat.
zh: 这时她突然注意到丈夫的帽子上有个大洞。

text: 'And how can you explain that?' she asked.
'Oh, that,' said the Ambassador.
'Someone fired a shot through my office window.
zh: “有人朝我办公室的窗户开了一枪。

text: Accurate, don't you think?
Fortunately, I wasn't wearing it at the time.
zh: 幸好当时我没戴着帽子。

text: If I had been, I would not have been able to get home for lunch.'
zh: 要是戴着的话,我就没法回家吃午饭了。”
